display.purpose: Hero brand titles and main page headers.

shadows.name: active
shadows.purpose: Applied to hovered items and modals.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Standard action button with slight scale-down effect.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #2563eb; color: #fff; padding: 12px 24px; font-weight: 600; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; border: none; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #1d4ed8; transform: scale(0.98); }

components.name: Standard Card
components.description: Base card container for stats and forms.
components.html:
Room Status

Room 101 is ready.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 8px; padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px 0 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05); transition: all 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-card:hover { border-color: #cbd5e1; box-shadow: 0 10px 15px -3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.03); } .ds-card-title { margin: 0 0 8px 0; color: #0f172a; font-weight: 700; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; } .ds-card-content { margin: 0; color: #334155; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; }

narrative.overview: This design system serves as a clean, highly structured administrative workspace optimized for fast-paced hospitality operations. It prioritizes information density and typographic hierarchy, ensuring front-desk clerks and managers can consume metrics, manage room statuses, and finalize check-out guest bookings with zero friction.
keyCharacteristics: High legibility through contrast and dark-neutral colors.
Functional status colors mapping directly to room and booking states.
Snappy interactions guided by micro-transitions and clear hover feedback.

rules.name: The Color Contrast Rule
rules.body: Primary body text must always preserve a >= 4.5:1 contrast ratio against the background. Muted gray text is strictly forbidden on colored surfaces.

rules.name: The Semantic Status Rule
rules.body: Status colors (Green for Available/Success, Red for Occupied/Danger, Yellow for Reserved/Warning, Blue for Cleaning/Info) must maintain a constant semantic mapping across all tables, badges, and filters.

rules.name: The Uppercase Eyebrow Restriction Rule
rules.body: Small, uppercase uppercase tracked eyebrows are prohibited as generic section titles.

rules.name: The Border-or-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Never combine a strong 1px border with a soft drop shadow on the same card or container. Choose clean borders at rest, and promote to shadows on hover.

dos: Do use semantic badge colors to represent state (e.g. green for Available / checked-in, red for Occupied / danger).
Do maintain a consistent 6px corner radius for inputs and buttons, and 8px/12px for cards.
Do ensure all form labels are positioned directly above the input fields.
donts: Don't use colored side-stripe borders (e.g. border-left-width: 4px) to designate item status or highlight state.
Don't use gradient text or decorative glassmorphism.
